heading-scale.name: heading-scale
heading-scale.accessibilityNotes: Always use a real semantic heading element (h1-h6) so the document outline is navigable. Don't pick a heading level for visual size alone — pair the correct heading level with a utility size class. Only one h1 per page (the hero); section titles are h2; cards/sub-sections are h3.
heading-scale.idiomaticUsage:
<>

Display heading

Section heading

Sub-section heading

Card title
heading-scale.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Display heading

Section heading

Sub-section heading

Card title

button-size-variants.name: button-size-variants
anatomy: .btn-primary (modifier — applies lime fill + evergreen text; stacks with size class)
inferredProps: as?: 'a' | 'button'  // marketing pages tend to use <a>
button-size-variants.accessibilityNotes: Use <a> only when the action navigates to a URL; use <button type="button"> for in-page actions. The pill radius is purely visual — focus rings must remain visible (do not remove default browser outline without replacing it with a 2px ring in --color-border-brand-secondary-default). Maintain 44x44px minimum hit target on touch surfaces — .btn-md and .btn-lg satisfy this; pad .btn with extra vertical space when used on mobile.
button-size-variants.idiomaticUsage: <> Sign up Request a demo Get started
button-size-variants.idiomaticUsageHtml: Sign up Request a demo Get started

partner-logo-tile.name: partner-logo-tile
inferredProps: href?: string  // wraps tile in <a> when present
partner-logo-tile.accessibilityNotes: Every logo image must have descriptive alt text (the partner name). If the tile links to a partner page, wrap in an <a> with an accessible name so screen readers announce both the destination and the partner. Decorative duplicate logos in a marquee should set alt="" and aria-hidden="true".

testimonial-card.name: testimonial-card
anatomy: .testimonial-card (rounded surface — radius-300, cream or peach background, 32px padding)
inferredProps: author: { name: string; role?: string; company?: string; avatarSrc?: string }
testimonial-card.accessibilityNotes: Use <blockquote> for the quote and <cite> (or a footer/figcaption) for the attribution so screen readers announce it as a quotation. If the card is part of a carousel (Slick slider), the wrapping <ul> needs role="region" with an accessible label and the slides should be reachable by keyboard arrow keys (Slick handles this when configured with `accessibility: true`).

case-study-card.name: case-study-card
anatomy: .case-study-card (large tinted card — radius-400/24px, 32px padding, peach/sand/cream tones)
.case-study-card__cta (text link — 'Read the story' with right-arrow icon)
inferredProps: href: string  // links to the full case study
case-study-card.accessibilityNotes: Wrap the whole tile in an <a> so the headline and metric are part of the same accessible link (avoid 'click here' style nested links). If using multiple cards in a grid, give each <a> a unique aria-label combining title + outcome. Decorative customer logos should have alt="" and aria-hidden="true" since the title repeats the company name.
case-study-card.idiomaticUsage:
Professional Services

How Elevate closed their books 2 weeks faster every month

2 wks faster monthly close
Read the story
case-study-card.idiomaticUsageHtml:

How Elevate closed their books 2 weeks faster every month

2 wks faster monthly close
Read the story

compact-card.name: compact-card
compact-card.accessibilityNotes: Use an h3 (inside a section with an h2) for the title so the heading order is preserved. Decorative icons should be aria-hidden. If the card is purely informational, no role is needed; if it links somewhere, wrap in an <a> rather than attaching onClick.
compact-card.idiomaticUsage:
Same-day ACH

Move money to suppliers without waiting for the standard window to settle.
compact-card.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Same-day ACH

Move money to suppliers without waiting for the standard window to settle.

content-block-zigzag.name: content-block-zigzag
anatomy: .flex-row-desktop (image left, copy right on desktop; stacks on mobile)
.flex-row-reverse-desktop (image right, copy left on desktop; stacks on mobile)
inferredProps: media: { src: string; alt: string } | ReactNode
reverse?: boolean  // true → image on right
content-block-zigzag.accessibilityNotes: Use a real <section> with an h2 as the title so each block contributes to the document outline. Order in DOM source should be copy-then-media (so screen readers and reading order get the headline first), then use CSS flex `flex-direction: row-reverse` to visually flip when `reverse` is true — don't reorder the DOM.
content-block-zigzag.idiomaticUsage:
Accounts Payable

Approve and pay vendors in one place.

Bring invoice approvals, payment scheduling, and accounting sync into a single workflow.

Learn more
content-block-zigzag.idiomaticUsageHtml:

spend-feature-block.name: spend-feature-block
anatomy: .spend-feature-block (dark gradient feature panel — 32px padding, 24px radius, dark golden noisy gradient bg)
.spend-feature-block__icon (lime Phosphor icon on the dark surface)
spend-feature-block.accessibilityNotes: On dark surfaces, body copy must be cream or lime (--color-background-alt or --color-text-brand-primary) to maintain 4.5:1 contrast. Headings inside should be h3 (assuming the enclosing section has its own h2). Decorative noise gradient is purely visual — no extra ARIA needed.
spend-feature-block.idiomaticUsage:
Cards employees actually like

Issue virtual or physical cards with built-in limits, vendor controls, and instant receipt capture.
spend-feature-block.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Cards employees actually like

Issue virtual or physical cards with built-in limits, vendor controls, and instant receipt capture.

perks-list-item.name: perks-list-item
anatomy: Optional inline Phosphor icon as list marker
perks-list-item.accessibilityNotes: Use a real <ul><li> structure so screen readers announce 'list, N items'. Don't replace bullets with images — if you use Phosphor icons as markers, give the <ul> role="list" (some browsers drop the implicit role when list-style: none is applied) and mark the icons aria-hidden so the perk text is what screen readers read.
perks-list-item.idiomaticUsage:
• Unlimited PTO
  • Health, dental, and vision
  • Annual learning stipend
perks-list-item.idiomaticUsageHtml:
• Unlimited PTO
  • Health, dental, and vision
  • Annual learning stipend

value-card.name: value-card
value-card.accessibilityNotes:
Inside a 'Our values' section (h2), each value title is an h3 for proper heading order. Body copy stays as a

. If decorative icons are added, mark them aria-hidden.
value-card.idiomaticUsage:
Curiosity

We ask better questions of our customers, our data, and ourselves — then we share what we learn.
value-card.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Curiosity

We ask better questions of our customers, our data, and ourselves — then we share what we learn.

hero-h1-display.name: hero-h1-display
anatomy: Optional inline span for color-swap accent (e.g., lime keyword on dark evergreen hero)
inferredProps: children: ReactNode  // headline copy, may contain an accent <span>
hero-h1-display.accessibilityNotes: This must be the only <h1> on the page. Use a real <h1> element so screen readers announce the page topic immediately. Hero headlines on dark evergreen surfaces need at least 4.5:1 contrast — both cream (--color-background-alt) and lime (--color-text-brand-primary) clear that bar on --color-background-fill-brand-secondary.
hero-h1-display.idiomaticUsage: Banking built for small businesses.
hero-h1-display.idiomaticUsageHtml: Banking built for small businesses.

disclaimer-list.name: disclaimer-list
anatomy: ul.disclaimer-list (small print container, ~30px left padding for hanging numerals)
li (BasisGrotesque 14px/400, cream tone on dark surface)
inferredProps: items: ReactNode[]  // each item may contain inline links to legal copy
disclaimer-list.accessibilityNotes: Disclaimers are legally meaningful — keep them as real <ol> or <ul> so assistive tech preserves the numbering. Inline links inside disclaimers must be color-distinguishable AND underlined (color alone fails WCAG when text-color-link is the only signal on a dark surface). Tone-down only the font size, never the color contrast.
disclaimer-list.idiomaticUsage:
• Banking services provided by Thread Bank, Member FDIC.
  • Relay is a financial technology company, not a bank.
  • Yield rates current as of publication and subject to change — see our yield disclosures.
disclaimer-list.idiomaticUsageHtml:
• Banking services provided by Thread Bank, Member FDIC.
  • Relay is a financial technology company, not a bank.
  • Yield rates current as of publication and subject to change — see our yield disclosures.

sidebar-nav.name: sidebar-nav
anatomy: .sidebar-nav (vertical column — 240px wide, full viewport height, cream surface, subtle right border)
inferredProps: currentPath: string  // for marking the active item
org: { id: string; name: string; logoSrc?: string }
items: Array<{ href: string; label: string; icon: PhosphorIconName }>
collapsed?: boolean  // narrow rail mode showing icons only
sidebar-nav.accessibilityNotes: Wrap in a <nav> landmark with aria-label="Primary" so screen-reader users can jump to it. The active item should have aria-current="page". Keyboard users should be able to Tab through items in DOM order; do not trap focus. When collapsed to a rail, give each icon link a visible tooltip AND an aria-label so the label remains discoverable.
sidebar-nav.idiomaticUsage:
• Accounts
  • Payments
  • Cards
sidebar-nav.idiomaticUsageHtml:
• Accounts
  • Payments
  • Cards

sidebar-nav-item.name: sidebar-nav-item
inferredProps: badge?: { count: number } | { dot: true }  // optional notification indicator
sidebar-nav-item.accessibilityNotes: Use an <a> with aria-current="page" when active so assistive tech announces the current location. Icon is decorative — aria-hidden. If a numeric badge is present, give it an aria-label like '3 unread' so screen readers don't just read the number out of context. Maintain 44px minimum height for touch targets when used on mobile rail.
sidebar-nav-item.idiomaticUsage: Expenses 3
sidebar-nav-item.idiomaticUsageHtml: Expenses 3

org-selector.name: org-selector
inferredProps: org: { id: string; name: string; logoSrc?: string }
onOpen: () => void  // opens the org-switcher Radix Popover/Dropdown
expanded?: boolean  // when popover is open (mirrors aria-expanded)
org-selector.accessibilityNotes: This is a button that toggles a menu — use <button type="button"> with aria-haspopup="menu", aria-expanded={open ? 'true' : 'false'}, and aria-controls pointing at the menu's id. Caret icon is decorative (aria-hidden). The org name + 'organization selector' should form the accessible name, e.g., aria-label="Switch organization, currently Acme Co."

page-shell.name: page-shell
anatomy: .page-shell__recaptcha (grecaptcha badge, bottom-right, visually-hidden when policy met)
page-shell.accessibilityNotes: Use <main> for the content column so screen readers can land on it via the 'main content' landmark. Provide a 'Skip to main content' link before the sidebar — visible only on focus — so keyboard users can bypass the nav. The Toastify region should be a polite live region (Toastify handles this by default via aria-live="polite").
page-shell.idiomaticUsage:
Skip to main content
{children}
page-shell.idiomaticUsageHtml: Skip to main content
page-shell.framework: Radix UI Themes layout utilities (rt-flex, rt-flex-row) + CSS Modules navbar

account-tile.name: account-tile
anatomy: .account-tile (card surface — cream bg, 12px radius, subtle border, 24px padding)
inferredProps: href?: string  // wraps tile in <a> for navigation
account-tile.accessibilityNotes: If the tile navigates, use an <a> wrapping the content (not onClick on a div). Use <dl><dt>{name}</dt><dd>{balance}</dd></dl> semantics inside so the metric pairs read correctly. Balance must be tabular-nums so live updates don't reflow the layout. Kebab menu is a separate <button> with aria-label="Account options" — stop click propagation if the tile is also a link.

Balance

Checking · •••4821
account-tile.idiomaticUsageHtml:

data-table.name: data-table
data-table.domSignature: div.tableWrapper > div.cardTableRoot > table > thead > tr.rt-bg-inherit > th.rt-border-b-100.rt-border-color-sand-300 + tbody > tr.last:rt-border-b-100 > td.rt-border-b-100.rt-border-color-sand-300
anatomy: .data-table__row (tr — 1px bottom border in --color-border-subtle / sand-300; last row no border)
inferredProps: columns: Array<{ id: string; header: string; align?: 'left' | 'right'; sortable?: boolean }>
data-table.accessibilityNotes: Use a real <table> with <thead>/<tbody>/<th scope="col">. Sortable columns: the <th> contains a <button> with aria-sort="ascending" | "descending" | "none" on the <th> itself. Caption: provide a <caption className="rt-sr-only"> or aria-label on the <table> describing what the data is. Numeric columns must use tabular-nums + right alignment so the eye can scan values. When loading, render skeleton rows with role="row" + aria-hidden="true" so the screen reader doesn't announce them.
data-table.idiomaticUsage:
Cardholder Card Status
Maria Chen Virtual ••••4821 Active $1,248.50
data-table.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Cardholder Card Status
Maria Chen Virtual ••••4821 Active $1,248.50
data-table.framework: Radix UI Themes (rt-* utilities) + bespoke per-feature CSS Modules

side-drawer.name: side-drawer
anatomy: .side-drawer (right-anchored panel — fixed position, full-height, cream surface, 1px left border)
.side-drawer__header (sticky top — title, optional eyebrow, close button)
.side-drawer__body (scrollable middle — the form, detail view, or workflow)
.side-drawer__collapse-button (optional — small chevron on the left edge to collapse to rail)
inferredProps: modal?: boolean  // when true, backdrop + focus trap
side-drawer.accessibilityNotes: When modal, this is a dialog: role="dialog", aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby pointing at the title id. Trap focus inside while open; restore focus to the trigger on close. Escape key must close. The Close button needs an explicit aria-label (just an X icon isn't enough). When non-modal (docked), no dialog semantics needed — but the title should still be a real h2 and the close button still labeled.
side-drawer.idiomaticUsage:
Payment details

{children}
side-drawer.idiomaticUsageHtml: Payment details

form-field-app.name: form-field-app
anatomy: .form-field-app (vertical column — flex-col, gap-50, full width by default)
form-field-app.accessibilityNotes: Every input MUST have a programmatic label — use
form-field-app.idiomaticUsage: As it appears on your incorporation documents.
form-field-app.idiomaticUsageHtml: As it appears on your incorporation documents.

select-menu.name: select-menu
anatomy: Popper-positioned listbox of options on click (Radix UI Select)
inferredProps: options: Array<{ value: string; label: string }>
select-menu.accessibilityNotes: Use Radix UI Select (the headless primitive) — it ships with full keyboard support (typeahead, arrows, Home/End, Esc to close). If hand-rolling, the trigger needs role="combobox" + aria-haspopup="listbox" + aria-expanded; the listbox role="listbox"; options role="option" + aria-selected. Match the label to the trigger via htmlFor/id or aria-labelledby. Never replace with a styled <div> that breaks form submission — keep a real <select> hidden behind the Radix UI render if needed.
select-menu.framework: CSS Modules (Select-module) likely wrapping Radix UI Select primitive

tab-bar.name: tab-bar
anatomy: .tab-bar (flex-row container — sits below the page-header, has a subtle bottom border)
.tab-bar__tab (button or link — px 12px, py 8px, bottom border 2px transparent)
inferredProps: tabs: Array<{ id: string; label: string; href?: string; count?: number }>
onSelect?: (id: string) => void  // for button-based tabs
as?: 'links' | 'buttons'  // links for URL-routed tabs, buttons for in-page
tab-bar.accessibilityNotes: When tabs control in-page panels, use ARIA tabs: role="tablist" on the container, role="tab" + aria-selected + aria-controls on each tab, role="tabpanel" + aria-labelledby on each panel; arrow keys move focus between tabs. When tabs are real URL routes (e.g., ?tab=invoices), use simple <a> with aria-current="page" and skip the tablist roles — search engines and back-button history work naturally.

card-product.name: card-product
anatomy: .card-product (card display — flex column, cream surface, 12px radius, 16-24px padding)
.card-product__image (the visual card art — typically 320x200 with rounded corners)
inferredProps: name: string  // cardholder or card nickname
card-product.accessibilityNotes: The card image should have a descriptive alt like 'Virtual Visa ending in 4821' — never alt="card". When the status is frozen/pending/expired, surface it as a status-pill next to the meta line (color alone isn't enough). Mask the full PAN even visually — always show only the last 4.
card-product.idiomaticUsage: Maria Chen Virtual · •••4821
card-product.idiomaticUsageHtml: Maria Chen Virtual · •••4821

payment-type-card.name: payment-type-card
anatomy: .payment-type-card (selectable card surface — cream, 12px radius, subtle border)
.payment-type-card__trigger (button covering most of the card — opens detail or starts flow)
payment-type-card.accessibilityNotes: These behave like a radio group when the user is choosing how to pay — use role="radiogroup" on the wrapper, role="radio" + aria-checked on each card, and arrow-key navigation between them. If they're more like quick actions, use plain <button>s. Always include both label AND fee/ETA in the accessible name so screen readers convey cost trade-offs.

app-button.name: app-button
anatomy: .app-button (in-app button — 8px radius, NOT pill; 12-16px padding; flat or outlined)
app-button.accessibilityNotes: Always type="button" unless inside a form that submits on Enter. Disabled buttons MUST still be discoverable by screen readers (use aria-disabled and visually grey them) rather than fully removed. When loading, set aria-busy="true" and replace the icon with a spinner, keeping the label readable. Focus ring must be visible — never `outline: none` without a replacement.
app-button.idiomaticUsage:
<>
  <button type="button" className="app-button app-button--primary rt-inline-flex rt-items-center rt-gap-50 rt-px-200 rt-py-100 rt-rounded-200 rt-bg-color-fill-brand-primary rt-text-color-brand-secondary rt-font-basis rt-text-100 rt-font-semibold rt-border-0 rt-cursor-pointer focus-visible:rt-outline focus-visible:rt-outline-2 focus-visible:rt-outline-color-border-brand-secondary-default">
    <i className="ph ph-plus" aria-hidden="true" />
    Add account
  </button>
  <button type="button" className="app-button app-button--secondary rt-inline-flex rt-items-center rt-gap-50 rt-px-200 rt-py-100 rt-rounded-200 rt-bg-transparent rt-border-100 rt-border-solid rt-border-color-border-default rt-text-color-default rt-font-basis rt-text-100 rt-font-med rt-cursor-pointer">Cancel</button>
  <button type="button" className="app-button app-button--ghost rt-inline-flex rt-items-center rt-gap-50 rt-px-150 rt-py-100 rt-rounded-200 rt-bg-transparent rt-border-0 rt-text-color-subtle rt-font-basis rt-text-100 rt-cursor-pointer hover:rt-bg-color-background-surface-brand-secondary hover:rt-text-color-default"><i className="ph ph-funnel" aria-hidden="true" />Filter</button>
</>
app-button.framework: CSS Modules (Button-module) — base composed with feature-specific module classes

sidebar-section.name: sidebar-section
sidebar-section.accessibilityNotes: When used as a docked side panel (not a modal), it doesn't need dialog semantics. When it overlays/blocks the main canvas, escalate to role="dialog" with aria-modal="true" and focus trapping (see side-drawer). The title should be an h2 (since the page h1 is elsewhere). Provide a visible Close button labeled 'Close panel' for screen readers.
sidebar-section.idiomaticUsage:
Filters

{children}
sidebar-section.idiomaticUsageHtml: Filters
sidebar-section.framework: CSS Modules (Sidebar-module). Composes with feature panels.

page-header.name: page-header
anatomy: .page-header (flex-row — title block on the left, action buttons on the right, 32px bottom margin)
inferredProps: eyebrow?: string  // breadcrumb tail or section label
page-header.accessibilityNotes: The title MUST be the single <h1> of the page — assistive tech relies on that to announce 'you are on X page'. If breadcrumbs are present, render them above the header in a <nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">. Action buttons should appear after the title in DOM order even when they're visually pulled right.
page-header.idiomaticUsage:
Cards

Issue and manage virtual and physical cards.
page-header.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Cards

Issue and manage virtual and physical cards.

avatar.name: avatar
anatomy: .avatar (img element OR a span fallback wrapper around initials)
span.avatar--fallback (initials on a tinted background when no image)
inferredProps: src?: string  // image URL — if missing, initials render
name: string  // used for alt text and initials fallback
tone?: 'sand' | 'lime' | 'evergreen'  // initials background when no image
avatar.accessibilityNotes: If the avatar appears alongside the person's name in the same UI block, the image is decorative — set alt="" so screen readers don't read the name twice. If the avatar is the only identifier (e.g., a presence dot in a header), give it a real alt with the person's name. Initials fallback should be aria-hidden when accompanied by the name elsewhere.

horizontal-grid.name: horizontal-grid
anatomy: .horizontal-grid (CSS grid — auto-fill columns with minmax, 16-24px gap)
Or horizontally scrollable rt-flex variant for marquees
Children: any card primitive (account-tile, kpi-tile, payment-type-card)
inferredProps: children: ReactNode  // typically a list of cards
scrollable?: boolean  // true = horizontal scroller instead of wrapping grid
horizontal-grid.accessibilityNotes: If the grid contains an actual list of items, render as <ul role="list"> with <li> children so screen readers announce a count. If horizontally scrollable on touch, ensure the container has tabIndex=0 so keyboard users can scroll with arrow keys, and label it with aria-label (e.g., 'Account cards').

accounting-rules-sidebar.name: accounting-rules-sidebar
anatomy: Composes side-drawer chrome with a rules-builder body
.accounting-rules-sidebar__footer (delete on the left, cancel + save on the right)
inferredProps: rule?: { id: string; name: string; conditions: Condition[]; actions: Action[] }  // omit for new
accounting-rules-sidebar.accessibilityNotes: Inherits all side-drawer modal semantics (role="dialog", aria-modal, focus trap). Group conditions and actions in <fieldset> with <legend> so screen readers announce the two halves of the rule. Delete is a destructive action — confirm via a secondary dialog rather than a single click; the button should have aria-label="Delete rule" and visual --color-background-fill-critical styling.

Then apply {/* form-field-app rows */}
accounting-rules-sidebar.idiomaticUsageHtml:

team-row.name: team-row
anatomy: td.team-row__access-popover-trigger (button, opens access permissions Popover — Phosphor ph-caret-down)
inferredProps: member: { id: string; name: string; email: string; avatarSrc?: string; role: 'owner' | 'admin' | 'member'; status?: 'active' | 'invited' | 'suspended' }
team-row.accessibilityNotes: Person cell should pair the avatar (alt="" since name follows) with the name as visible text and email as a smaller line — screen reader gets 'Maria Chen, maria@acme.co'. The role/permissions trigger is a <button> with aria-haspopup="menu" and aria-expanded; menu actions (Change role, Remove) live inside the popover. Pending invites should surface a status-pill so visually-impaired users know the row isn't yet a real member.
team-row.idiomaticUsage:
{member.name} {member.email}
Admin
team-row.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Maria Chen maria@acme.co
Admin
team-row.framework: Composes data-table row + CSS Modules (TeamMembersTable-module, TeamManagement-module)

feature-carousel.name: feature-carousel
inferredProps: slides: Array<{ id: string; image: string; alt: string; caption?: string }>
feature-carousel.accessibilityNotes: Carousels are accessibility minefields. Use role="region" with aria-roledescription="carousel" and aria-label on the container. Each slide gets role="group" + aria-roledescription="slide" + aria-label="X of Y". Arrow buttons need aria-label="Previous slide"/"Next slide" (icon alone is not enough). NEVER auto-play without a visible Pause control — autoplay is a WCAG 2.2.2 fail otherwise. Hidden slides should be aria-hidden so screen readers don't read them.
feature-carousel.idiomaticUsage:
{slides.map((s, i) => (
{s.caption &&

{s.caption}

}
))}
feature-carousel.idiomaticUsageHtml: Send branded invoices in seconds.
feature-carousel.notes: Inferred from the single-page extraction (.FeatureCarousel-module__arrow on /get-paid). Authored composition assumes a typical horizontal slider pattern with prev/next arrows; the live implementation may use a third-party library (e.g., embla, swiper) under the hood. Markup shown is a reasonable accessibility baseline for hand-rolled carousels at Relay.

kpi-tile.name: kpi-tile
kpi-tile.domSignature: div.kpi-tile > (span.kpi-tile__label + span.kpi-tile__value + div.kpi-tile__delta > (i.ph + span)) + optional span.kpi-tile__caption

variants.name: inverse-on-dark
variants.notes: For KPI tiles placed on Relay's dark evergreen hero or section backgrounds (--color-background-fill-brand-secondary).

anatomy: .kpi-tile__delta (trend pill — radius-round, Phosphor arrow + delta% + period label)
inferredProps: delta?: { value: number; direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'flat'; period?: string }
kpi-tile.accessibilityNotes: Wrap label + value semantically (e.g., <dl><dt>{label}</dt><dd>{value}</dd></dl>) so screen readers announce the metric name with its number. Give the delta pill an aria-label like 'up 12.4% vs last month'; mark the Phosphor icon aria-hidden. Use tabular-nums on the value so a number that ticks live doesn't cause layout jitter. Color alone never carries delta direction — always pair with the arrow icon.
kpi-tile.idiomaticUsage:
Total balance $248,392
+12.4% vs last month
kpi-tile.idiomaticUsageHtml:
Total balance $248,392
+12.4% vs last month
kpi-tile.framework: Radix UI Themes utilities + Phosphor icons. Works in React (.tsx) and plain HTML (.html) — see idiomaticUsage / idiomaticUsageHtml. No JS dependency.
kpi-tile.notes: Curated addition — not extracted from a live Relay page. Designed to compose with the existing card aesthetic (matches pricing-card radius/surface, compact-card padding) and Relay's typographic scale. Use as the canonical 'big number + trend' primitive on dashboards. For the sparkline variant, see kpi-tile-sparkline.

kpi-tile-sparkline.name: kpi-tile-sparkline

variants.name: default-on-light-brand
variants.notes: Use brand-secondary stroke when the metric is not directional (e.g., a balance over time where 'up' isn't inherently positive).

anatomy: .kpi-tile__header (label + value + delta stacked vertically, same as base kpi-tile)
.kpi-tile__spark (full-width Recharts AreaChart, height 64px, no axes, no grid, no tooltip)
inferredProps: delta?: { value: number; direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'flat'; period?: string }
data: { x: string | number; y: number }[]
kpi-tile-sparkline.accessibilityNotes: Sparkline is decorative — set aria-hidden on the chart SVG and rely on the label + value + delta for the accessible name. If the sparkline conveys information the delta does not (e.g., volatility), provide an aria-describedby pointing to a visually-hidden summary. Keep tabular-nums on the value.
kpi-tile-sparkline.idiomaticUsage:
'use client'; import { AreaChart, Area, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';
30-day inflow $84,210
+8.2%
kpi-tile-sparkline.idiomaticUsageHtml:
30-day inflow $84,210
+8.2%

dependencies.html: None — pure inline SVG. The optional 25-line scaler (idiomaticUsageHtmlScaler) handles dynamic data.

kpi-tile-sparkline.framework: Two modes: React (Recharts v3 client-only) or plain HTML (inline SVG, zero JS dependency). HTML mode is the preferred path for teammates working in .html files.
kpi-tile-sparkline.notes: Curated addition. Sparkline uses Recharts AreaChart with explicit 64px height (ResponsiveContainer needs a defined parent height — see dashboard-chart gotchas). Each tone (positive/negative/brand) uses its own linearGradient id; ensure ids are unique per page or scope them with a generated suffix to avoid SVG defs collisions.

dashboard-chart.name: dashboard-chart

variants.name: line
variants.useCase: Time series with one or more continuous metrics (balance over time, runway, daily revenue).

variants.name: area
variants.useCase: Time series with emphasis on cumulative magnitude (cash on hand, total spend).

variants.name: bar
variants.useCase: Categorical breakdown (top vendors, spend by category, weekly volume).

variants.name: donut
variants.useCase: Composition / share of total (account mix, expense categories).

tokenBridge.purpose: Bind Recharts series colors to Relay tokens via the shadcn/ui ChartContainer CSS-var pattern.
tokenBridge.usage: Pass stroke/fill={`var(--chart-${n})`} on Line/Area/Bar/Cell elements. Avoid wrapping in hsl() — Relay tokens are hex, and a wrapper breaks Recharts v3 color parsing.

inferredProps: series: { key: string; label: string; tokenVar: string }[]
showGrid?: boolean  // default true except sparkline
showLegend?: boolean  // default true for donut, false otherwise
showTooltip?: boolean  // default true except sparkline
ariaLabel: string  // required — summarizes what the chart shows
dashboard-chart.accessibilityNotes: Charts are SVG and largely opaque to screen readers. Provide a required ariaLabel summarizing the metric, range, and direction (e.g., 'Total balance over the last 12 months, trending up'). For data-critical charts, render a visually-hidden <table> sibling with the same data (caption + thead + tbody) — Recharts has no built-in equivalent. Ensure series rely on more than color to be distinguishable: use line style (solid vs dashed), bar pattern, or direct labels for users with color-vision deficiencies.
dashboard-chart.idiomaticUsage: 'use client'; import { LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

react.optional: shadcn/ui chart.tsx primitives — copy from https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart for ChartContainer / ChartTooltipContent / ChartLegendContent wrappers if you want the full config-driven API. They work cleanly with Relay tokens once chart-N vars are bridged.

html.interactive: Chart.js ^4 (recommended, broad familiarity, MIT) OR ApexCharts ^3 (SVG-based, matches sparkline rendering). Single <script> tag, no build step.

htmlMode.purpose: For .html files (no React, no JSX). Two paths depending on interactivity needs.

staticSvg.useWhen: Server-rendered or static dashboards — no tooltips, no zoom, no live updates. Zero JS dependency.
staticSvg.approach: Inline <svg> with <polyline>, <path>, and <linearGradient>. Bind colors via stroke="var(--color-...)" attributes so they pick up Relay tokens automatically. The pattern in kpi-tile-sparkline.idiomaticUsageHtml works directly for sparklines, simple line/area, and short bar charts (use <rect> with token fills).
staticSvg.helper: kpi-tile-sparkline.idiomaticUsageHtmlScaler is a 25-line function that converts a value array into the polyline/path strings — copy it once, reuse across charts.

interactive.why: Single <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4"></script> tag, MIT, no build step, broad community familiarity. Bridge Relay tokens at runtime via getComputedStyle.
interactive.alternative: ApexCharts — SVG-based (matches inline sparkline rendering), more chart types out of the box, slightly heavier.
interactive.skip: Recharts, shadcn ChartContainer, Tremor — all React-only. The idiomaticUsage example in this entry is React-only; use idiomaticUsageHtml for .html files.

gotchas: [React] Add 'use client' to every file that imports a Recharts component — Recharts cannot SSR.
[React] ResponsiveContainer needs an explicit parent height (px, vh, or aspect-ratio). Without it, the chart measures 0 on first paint and never renders.
[React] Use stroke={`var(--token)`} directly. Do NOT wrap in hsl() — Relay's tokens are hex; old shadcn snippets that use hsl(var(--chart-1)) will produce invalid colors.
[React] Recharts v3 deprecated <Cell> in favor of the shape prop on Bar/Pie. Use shape={({...}) => <rect ... />} for per-datapoint customization.
[Both] linearGradient ids must be unique per page. Scope them per component (e.g., id="spark-success-{uniqueId}") to avoid SVG defs collisions when multiple charts mount.
[React] Tooltip's contentStyle is inline (no className override). Style via the prop, not CSS modules.

[HTML] Read Relay tokens once with getComputedStyle().getPropertyValue('--color-...').trim() and cache the result — don't call it inside Chart.js animation callbacks. The .trim() is required (browsers return a leading space).
[HTML] Chart.js is canvas-based — it won't pick up CSS-var changes when the theme switches. If you support theme toggling, destroy + recreate the chart on theme change.
dashboard-chart.framework: Two modes: React (Recharts v3 + optional shadcn ChartContainer) or plain HTML (Chart.js / ApexCharts via <script> tag, tokens bridged via getComputedStyle). HTML mode is the preferred path for teammates working in .html files. Static sparklines and simple charts can use pure inline SVG with zero JS — see htmlMode.staticSvg.
dashboard-chart.notes: Curated addition — no chart primitive existed in extracted Relay surfaces. Single entry with variants per chart type (line/area/bar/donut) so the registry stays tight; sparkline lives in kpi-tile-sparkline since its anatomy is bound to the KPI tile shell. Theming uses the shadcn/ui ChartContainer + ChartConfig pattern (see https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart) which reads series colors from CSS custom properties — chosen specifically because it doesn't require Tailwind safelist entries for custom colors (the friction point that ruled out Tremor).

status-pill.name: status-pill

variants.name: success
variants.useCase: Payment posted, transfer complete, account active, KYC approved.

variants.name: warning
variants.useCase: Pending review, action required, expiring soon.

variants.name: critical
variants.useCase: Payment failed, account frozen, declined transaction.

anatomy: .status-pill__dot (optional 8px round, solid fill in tone color)
status-pill.accessibilityNotes: Status conveys state — never rely on color alone. The label text must describe the state ('Posted', 'Failed') so screen readers and color-blind users get the same information. The .status-pill__dot is decorative; mark it aria-hidden. If used inside a table cell, the cell's accessible name should include the status (the label text handles this naturally).
status-pill.idiomaticUsage: Posted
status-pill.idiomaticUsageHtml: Posted
status-pill.framework: Radix UI Themes utilities. Works in React (.tsx) and plain HTML (.html). No JS dependency.
status-pill.notes: Curated addition. Replaces ad-hoc status badges sprinkled across data-table cells. Each tone now maps cleanly to a real surface token (--color-background-surface-{success,warning,critical,info,default}) and a fill or text token. Warning uses --color-text-warning specifically since the warning fill fails AA contrast as text on light surfaces.

empty-state.name: empty-state

variants.name: in-card
variants.useCase: Inside a data-table, kpi-tile, or dashboard-chart when there's no data yet.

variants.name: full-page
variants.useCase: Full content area is empty (no accounts yet, no transactions in date range).

variants.name: error
variants.useCase: Something went wrong loading data (network error, permission denied).
variants.notes: Body and CTA stay default-toned; only the icon + title carry the critical signal so the layout stays calm.

inferredProps: icon: PhosphorIconName  // e.g. 'plus-circle' for 'create your first account'
cta?: { label: string; href: string } | { label: string; onClick: () => void }
empty-state.accessibilityNotes: If used to replace data content, set role="status" so screen readers announce the empty state when it appears after a load. The Phosphor icon is decorative — aria-hidden. The title should be a real heading (h3 inside a card, h2 for full-page) so users navigating by headings can locate it. If the CTA is the primary action, give it autoFocus so keyboard users can act without tabbing.
empty-state.idiomaticUsage:
No transactions yet

Once you send a payment or receive a deposit, it'll show up here.

Send a payment
empty-state.idiomaticUsageHtml:
No transactions yet

Once you send a payment or receive a deposit, it'll show up here.

Send a payment
empty-state.framework: Radix UI Themes utilities + Phosphor icons. Works in React (.tsx) and plain HTML (.html). No JS dependency.
empty-state.notes: Curated addition. Choose the Phosphor icon to match the metric: 'receipt' for transactions, 'wallet' for accounts, 'chart-line' for analytics, 'users' for team, 'credit-card' for cards. Keep body copy under 2 sentences and present-tense.

skeleton.name: skeleton

variants.name: tile
variants.useCase: Loading state for kpi-tile and kpi-tile-sparkline — preserves layout to prevent CLS.
anatomy: .skeleton-bar.skeleton-bar--delta (16px tall, 30% width, rounded — placeholder for delta pill)

variants.name: chart
variants.useCase: Loading state for dashboard-chart — preserves chart height.
anatomy: .skeleton-chart (container — same dimensions as dashboard-chart, height required)
.skeleton-chart__xaxis (row of small bars across the bottom)

variants.name: table-row
variants.useCase: Loading state for data-table rows — render 3-5 of these inside the tbody.
anatomy: tr.skeleton-row > td > .skeleton-bar (one per column, height 16px, widths varied)
.skeleton (base — sand-300 → sand-200 linear-gradient with shimmer animation)
.skeleton-bar (skeleton sized like a line of text)
inferredProps: count?: number  // for table-row, how many rows to render (default 5)
skeleton.accessibilityNotes: Skeletons should NOT be announced to screen readers — wrap them in aria-hidden="true" and set role="status" on the parent container with an aria-label like 'Loading transactions'. Respect prefers-reduced-motion: at @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce), disable the shimmer keyframes (set animation: none) so the placeholder is static.
skeleton.idiomaticUsage: // Drop the keyframes once globally, then compose skeletons inline.
skeleton.framework: Pure CSS animation. Works in React (.tsx) and plain HTML (.html). No JS dependency.
skeleton.notes: Curated addition. Match the skeleton's bounding box to the loaded component's dimensions exactly — wrong sizes cause cumulative layout shift (CLS) when content arrives. The shimmer uses Relay's sand palette (--color-background-fill-default → --color-background-alt) so it reads as 'loading' without competing for attention. Always include the prefers-reduced-motion override.

background.surface.success.derivedFrom: Aliased to --color-background-surface-brand-secondary; visually identical for the success state.

text.warning.derivedFrom: --color-background-fill-warning (#fbb655) darkened ~30% for AA contrast as text on light surfaces. Use when warning copy or icons need to sit on the warning surface.

colors.curatedNotes: Status-tone surface and text tokens added to close gaps surfaced by status-pill and kpi-tile negative-delta. Not present in the live Relay CSS; derived from the corresponding fill tokens. The full extracted set in fullSetAt is unchanged.

notifications.notes: 41 --toastify-* vars exposed globally; Relay overrides toast background via Toastify__toast-theme--light. See full file for the complete enumeration.

marketing-page.name: marketing-page
marketing-page.framework: Radix UI Themes + custom Relay tokens

app-shell.name: app-shell
app-shell.containerWidth: Fills viewport; main content has 64px horizontal gutters.
app-shell.verticalRhythm: 64px top + 0px between page-header and first content row
app-shell.framework: Radix UI Themes layout utilities + CSS Modules per feature
app-shell.notes: No top-bar component — org switcher lives at the top of the sidebar-nav rather than across the top of the viewport.

app-content-table-view.name: app-content-table-view
app-content-table-view.containerWidth: Fills main content area inside app-shell
app-content-table-view.verticalRhythm: 24px between header and table; rows are 1px sand-300 separated
app-content-table-view.framework: CSS Modules per feature + Radix utilities

pill-cta.description: Brand-primary lime pill button with dark-green text, used as the dominant CTA across landing flows.

inverse-form.description: Dark evergreen (#002813) background with cream (#f4f1e7) text/borders for forms on dark heroes.

preheader-display-stack.description: Lime preheader label (24px/700 RadionB) above a large display heading inside .feature blocks.

partner-logo-grid.description: Grid of `.logo-container` off-white tiles (70px 20px padding, no radius) showcasing integration partners.

testimonial-carousel.description: Slick-based carousel with `.slider-testimonial.elevate` slides and `.slick-arrow-position` navigation.

marketing-hero-dark-with-pill-cta.description: Dark `.hero.main-gradient` or `.bg-green` background with `.c-white` H1 (64–100px) and a pill CTA via `.btn.btn-primary`.

transaction-list.description: Expenses/transactions/team/cards/payments pages: page-header + optional tab-bar + filters + data-table with sand-300 row separators, opens side-drawer on row click.

account-overview.description: Accounts dashboard: page-header + horizontal-grid (or vertical stack) of account-tiles with balance, name, type, action icons. MoveMoneyMenu trigger sits in the header.

settings-form-section.description: Settings pages: tab-bar across the top, then sections of form-field-app stacked vertically with section headings (RadionB 24px). Save action lives in a sticky bottom bar (inferred — not captured).

drawer-edit-flow.description: Click a row -> SideDrawer slides in from right with sticky header (title + close), scrollable body, sticky footer (Cancel + primary Save). Used for accounting rules, card details, transaction details, team-member access edits.

payment-type-picker.description: Send-money landing: horizontal row of clickable cards (ACH/Wire/Check/etc.), each a button with icon + bold label + body copy that launches the appropriate flow.
